name: PennyLane (Quantum ML)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Xanadu; PennyLane Cloud for hardware access

auth.notes: Local simulation: no auth; hardware backends (IBM, AWS Braket, Azure Quantum): credentials

performance.notes: Local simulation: fast; hardware backend: queue latency (minutes to hours)

agent_readiness.notes: Quantum machine learning framework from Xanadu. Differentiable quantum circuits (quantum backprop). Integrates with PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX for hybrid quantum-classical ML. Multiple backends: default.qubit (simulation), IBM Quantum, AWS Braket, Azure Quantum, real photonic hardware (Xanadu). VQE, QAOA, quantum neural networks. Gradients via parameter-shift rule. The dominant QML framework for research.

gotchas: Real quantum hardware has long queue times and high noise
Quantum ML advantage not demonstrated for most practical tasks yet
Steep learning curve (quantum computing + ML together)
Simulation scales poorly (2^n qubits in memory)
More research tool than production tool currently
best_when: Teams exploring quantum advantage for ML tasks
avoid_when: Production ML (classical ML far more practical currently)
Problems where classical solutions are well-established
summary: PennyLane is the leading quantum machine learning framework from Xanadu. Differentiable quantum circuits with PyTorch/TensorFlow/JAX integration. Multiple backends (simulation, IBM, AWS Braket, Azure). VQE, QAOA, quantum neural networks via parameter-shift gradients. The dominant QML framework for research. Practical quantum advantage over classical ML not yet demonstrated for most tasks.
